name: Field Hospital, Brooklyn Earthwork Interior
description: A shallow earthwork hollow behind the main parapet line, where the wounded from the August 27th fighting lie on straw in the mud. The groaning is continuous and low; the orderlies have been told to keep the men quiet but cannot enforce it.
atmosphericDetails: The smell of wound suppuration and wet straw, underlaid by the iron tang of old blood on cloth.
The low, involuntary groaning of men who have been lying in the mud for two days and know the army is leaving.
The whispered arguments of orderlies debating whether to carry stretcher cases to the slip without authorization.
The sound of a man calling for water in the dark, repeatedly, with no one answering.
